label: Laddering
intent: User wants to clarify what they actually want or why something matters
protocol: Ask 'What would having that give you?' on each stated want — ladder upward
Continue until the user names a terminal value (connection, freedom, meaning, security, etc.)
Reflect the value back without elaborating — let the user confirm or refine
Explore what life looks like when this value is honoured vs. thwarted
Link the original want back to the value: does it actually serve it?
opening_moves: What would having that give you?
And if you had that — what would that do for you?
deepening_questions: If you had that, what would be different?
What would you be able to do — or be — that you can't now?
Is there something even more fundamental underneath that?
do_not: Do not supply the terminal value — the user must name it
Do not jump to solutions before the value is clear
Do not validate the original want before laddering
exit_criteria: User names a terminal value and confirms it resonates; the ladder circles back on itself
